General. Welcome, Mr. Stark. Look forward to your weapons presentation next. Is it better to be feared or respected? And I say, is it too much to ask for both? With that in mind, I humbly present the crown jewel of Stark Industries' Freedom Line. It's the first missile system to incorporate our proprietary repulsor technology. They say the best weapon is one you never have to fire. I respect greed. I prefer the weapon you only have to fire once. That's how Dad did it. That's how America does it, and it's worked out pretty well so far. I'm sorry, this is the fun bee, the humdrum bee's thing. Nice job. See you back, bitch.I wouldn't do that if I were you. What the hell did you do to me? What I did? What I did life. I removed all the shrapnel I could. There's a lot left, and it's headed into your atrial septum. Here, want to see? Have a souvenir. Take a look. I've seen many wounds like that in my village. We call them the walking dead because it takes about a week for the barbs to reach the vital organs. What is this? That is an electromagnet hooked up to a car battery, and it's keeping the shrapnel from entering your heart. That's right, smile. We met once, you know, at a technical conference in Bern. Fine, great, Tony Stark, or are you going to do something about it? I should do anything they can to kill me, you, either way, and if they don't, I'll probably be dead in a week. Well then, this is a very important week for you, isn't it? This is gonna be my workstation. I want it well lit. I want these poplar tools. I need welding gear. I know what's in there. What they're looking for. Masakin! Relax. The bow and arrow once was the pinnacle of weapons technology. It allowed the great Genghis Khan to rule from the Pacific to the Ukraine, an empire twice the size of Alexander the Great. And four times the size of the Roman Empire. But today, whoever holds the latest Stark weapons rules these lands. And soon, that'll be my turn.
